content.text:
I want you to ingest 11 Computer Vision lecture decks (10–20) into my Obsidian vault, embedding diagrams from the slides.

Vault: /Users/mattporteous/Obsidian/School
Read CLAUDE.md first — it defines the slide-ingest workflow plus two image-source conventions
(slides-png/ for full-slide screenshots, images/ for extracted bitmaps).

Before processing anything, please:

1. Verify the image pre-extraction is complete by running:
     for d in 30-Sources/Computer-Vision/slides-png/*/; do
       printf "%s  %d slides\n" "$(basename "$d")" "$(ls "$d" | wc -l)"
     done
   The previous attempt was Ctrl+C'd; only lecture 10 has ~42 slides and the rest are missing.
   If incomplete, run bin/ingest-slides.sh Computer-Vision to finish (asks no input; takes ~5 min).

2. Confirm with me on these open decisions before writing any notes:
   a. The two templates in 99-Templates/ (lecture.md, concept.md) are minimal live-capture stubs
      and don't match the rich Overview/Key concepts/Equations/Diagrams/Open questions structure
      the slide-ingest workflow uses. Should they be updated, or stay separate?
   b. Default image source: slides-png/ (easy, includes branding) vs images/ (clean figures, but
      need PDF inspection to identify which extracted file = which slide figure). I'd default to
      slides-png/.
   c. Where do diagrams go: lecture notes only, or also in concept notes? (Atomic concepts feel
      like at most one diagram each.)

3. Then for each lecture 10 → 20 in numeric order:
   - Read the PDF in 30-Sources/Computer-Vision/pdf/{NN}_{name}.pdf
   - Search 20-Concepts/ first; extend existing notes instead of duplicating
   - Show a brief plan (lecture path + concept names + flashcard count + diagrams to embed)
     then proceed; no need to wait for "go" between lectures
   - Add [[wikilinks]] back to earlier-lecture concepts wherever this lecture builds on them
   - Embed slide images using `![[../../30-Sources/Computer-Vision/slides-png/{lecture-name}/slide-NN.png]]`
     with a one-line *Figure: ... (slide N)* caption underneath

4. At the end, summary table: lecture | concepts created | concepts extended | flashcards | total links

The vault is currently empty for this batch — no prior CV notes exist, so concepts you create
in early lectures will become the link targets for later ones.

Start by reading CLAUDE.md and the first PDF after answering the questions above.
I'll start by verifying the image pre-extraction state and surveying what's there.

rawInput.description: Extract slide PNGs for all CV lectures

content.text: I see a problem — `bin/ingest-slides.sh` only extracts embedded bitmaps via `pdfimages` to the `images/` folder. It does NOT generate `slides-png/` (full-page renders). Let me check what naming convention slides-png uses and find the right tool.

content.title: which pdftoppm magick convert 2>/dev/null; pdftoppm -h 2>&1 | head -5

rawInput.command: which pdftoppm magick convert 2>/dev/null; pdftoppm -h 2>&1 | head -5

content.text:
Found the issue — let me brief you fully so we can decide before any rendering or writing.

## Pre-flight findings

**Slide-PNG extraction:** Only lecture 10 has `slides-png/` (42 slides). `bin/ingest-slides.sh` does **not** generate `slides-png/` — it only runs `pdfimages` to populate `images/` (extracted bitmaps). The `slides-png/10_fourier_transform2/` folder must have been produced by a separate one-off render that wasn't committed to the script. I have `pdftoppm` available and can render the missing 11–20 myself; rough estimate ~3–6 min at 120 dpi.

**Images extraction:** Only 10–13 have `images/` populated. The background `pdfimages` job I started will finish 14–20 (no output means it's done quickly with `set -e` succeeding silently — let me confirm).

**Vault state:** Empty for CV — no concept notes, no lecture notes, no review notes. Clean slate.

## My recommendations on your three open decisions

**(a) Templates** — Keep them separate. `99-Templates/lecture.md` is a live-capture stub (during-class engagement), while the slide-ingest output is a structured post-hoc reference artifact. Different purposes, different audiences (future-you vs in-class-you). Updating the template to match would lose the lightweight in-class form.

**(b) Default image source** — Default to `slides-png/` (full slide screenshots). Reasons: (1) trivially identifiable by slide number, (2) preserves caption + slide context, (3) `images/` extraction order doesn't correspond to slide order so identifying the right one requires opening the PDF for every embed. Use `images/` only when a specific clean diagram is materially better (e.g., a textbook figure I want isolated).

**(c) Diagrams in concept notes** — At most one representative slide per concept note, only when it adds something the prose can't. Most atomic concepts won't need one. The lecture note carries the visual narrative; concept notes carry the idea.

## What I'd do once you confirm

1. Render slides-png for lectures 11–20 with `pdftoppm -png -r 120` per PDF, naming `slide-NN.png` (zero-padded 2-digit).
2. Process lectures 10 → 20 in order, per CLAUDE.md slide-ingest workflow, with the brief plan-then-write cadence you described.
3. Track running counts and produce the summary table at end.

Two quick confirmations needed:

- **OK to render slides-png 11–20 with `pdftoppm`?** (writes ~400-600 PNGs to `30-Sources/Computer-Vision/slides-png/`)
- **Endorse a/b/c above?** (or override any)

Standing by.Background `pdfimages` job done (exit 0) — `images/` should now be populated for 14–20.

Awaiting your confirmation on the two questions above before rendering slides-png and starting the lecture ingest.
